context_history.content: I want to learn about: html. DO NOT greet me. Immediately start lesson Step 1 by explaining the core concept on the whiteboard.

HTML, or HyperText Markup Language, is the standard language used to create the structure of web pages. Think of it as the skeleton of a building; it defines where the walls, windows, and doors go, but doesn't handle the paint or furniture.

visual_content.detail: HTML (Skeleton), CSS (Skin/Style), JavaScript (Muscles/Action), The relationship between web technologies

context_history.content: HTML uses 'tags' to mark up content, usually coming in pairs: an opening tag and a closing tag. Closing tags are identical to opening tags but include a forward slash before the tag name to signify the end of a section.

visual_content.detail: <tag>, Content, </tag>, Anatomy of an HTML Element
